title: Developer successfully ports Linux to 1994 Sega 32X Genesis and MegaDrive expansion runs open-source OS on paltry 23MHz processors and 256KB of RAM
summary: A developer successfully ported Linux to the 1994 Sega 32X, a 23MHz 256KB machine. This achievement showcases the adaptability of open-source operating systems. The port is a testament to the developer's technical expertise.
insight: Open-source OS can thrive on low-end hardware with sufficient expertise.

title: Legendary Gravis Ultrasound sound card gets new open-source clone Beavis Ultrasound remake includes complete KiCad schematics, PCB layout, sample ROM, and more
summary: The Beavis Ultrasound project is an open-source remake of the legendary Gravis Ultrasound ISA soundcard, featuring complete KiCad schematics, PCB layout, sample ROM, and more.
insight: Reviving retro tech for modern enthusiasts and developers to experiment with.

title: CXMT close to matching Micron's memory capacity in 2026, research claims would put China on track to become world's second-largest DRAM producer
summary: CXMT is reportedly close to matching Micron's DRAM capacity, setting China on track to become the world's second-largest DRAM producer, surpassing South Korea.
insight: China's DRAM industry growth challenges South Korea's dominance.

title: Ukraine conducts record drone strike of 2,500km after 12-hour flight $55,000 unit made of plywood halts operations at Russia's largest gasoline producer
summary: Ukraine's FP-1 drones flew 2,500km over 12 hours to strike Omsk refinery.
insight: Ukraine showcases long-range drone capabilities against strategic targets.

title: PS5 emulation ramps up in response to Sony killing physical games PS5 titles now booting across different emulators with rapid community development for both 2D and 3D games
summary: PS5 emulation has accelerated with SharpEmu and KytyPS5 receiving significant updates, enabling 2D and 3D game emulation on PC. The rapid community development has made it possible to run several PS5 games across different emulators. This shift may impact the gaming industry.
insight: PS5 emulation gains pace, threatening Sony's game sales model.

title: US gov't allows Chinese telecom giant ZTE to purchase Nvidia H200 AI chips firm joins Alibaba, Tencent, and ByteDance in access to Hopper tech
summary: The US government has allowed ZTE to purchase restricted Nvidia H200 AI chips, but Chinese regulations may limit their impact.
insight: US tech export controls compromise remain a concern for future sales

title: Samsung 990 2TB SSD Review: New flash, familiar speeds
summary: The Samsung 990 2TB SSD offers newer flash but similar speeds to its predecessor, the 990 EVO Plus. Its power efficiency is mediocre. This storage device provides a familiar performance experience.
insight: New flash doesn't necessarily mean significant performance upgrades.

title: Elon Musk s Colossus 2 data center installed 59 natural gas turbines without permission, report claims thousands of tons of pollutants reportedly impact black communities in Mississippi already suffering from elevated lung disease rates
summary: Elon Musk's Colossus 2 data center installed 59 unpermitted natural gas turbines, allegedly emitting thousands of tons of pollutants. The surrounding communities, predominantly black, are already experiencing elevated lung disease rates. This has sparked a lawsuit and environmental concerns.
insight: Environmental injustice disproportionately affects vulnerable minority communities.

title: China claims chip exports nearly doubled to $177 billion in the first half of 2026 as memory prices surged 96% year-on-year increase inflated by hikes
summary: China's chip exports surged to $177 billion in the first half of 2026 due to a 96% year-on-year increase in memory prices, driven by global demand for AI hardware.
insight: Rising AI demand fuels China's booming semiconductor export market.

title: Intel invests $5.7 billion in Ireland fab aims to boost output of Xeon 6, next-gen Xeon products built on Intel 3 process
summary: Intel is investing $5.7 billion in its Irish fab to boost Xeon output.
insight: Boosting Xeon supply to meet growing demand in the data center market

title: Razer Blade 16 (2026) review: Competitive gaming performance and class-leading endurance
summary: The Razer Blade 16 offers exceptional gaming performance and impressive battery life. With a nearly $5,000 price tag, it is a significant investment. For serious gamers, this laptop delivers top-notch results.
insight: High-end gaming laptops now prioritize endurance and performance equally.

title: New York enacts one-year data center ban on projects larger than 50 megawatts first US state to implement moratorium; will also pursue repealing tax exemptions
summary: New York enacts a one-year moratorium on data centers over 50 MW, aiming to establish consistent standards and study environmental impacts. This pause will give the state time to create a Generic Environmental Impact Statement (GEIS). The move is part of a broader effort to reassess the tax exemptions provided to data centers.
insight: Regulatory scrutiny of data centers gains momentum nationwide first.

title: Nvidia slashes list of authorized customers in Asia in a bid to reduce AI chip smuggling, report claims company sent field inspectors, called customers to check if business is genuine after pressure from Washington
summary: Nvidia has significantly reduced its authorized customer list in Asia to combat AI chip smuggling. The remaining clients have undergone stricter verification, including physical inspections and user interviews. This move comes after Washington's pressure.
insight: Nvidia combats AI chip smuggling by verifying high-risk clients.

title: Drop's $130 desktop PC speakers are now just $23 save a massive 82% on these dual-orientation BMR1 V2 speakers
summary: Save 82% on Drop's BMR1 V2 speakers, now $23, for immersive desktop sound.
insight: Enhance your desktop audio experience with affordable high-quality speakers.

title: Retro Nintendo Switch 2 dock looks exactly like the Nintendo 64 and holds six Game Cards in its cartridge slot the 64BITDOCK is available now starting at $89
summary: The Retro Nintendo Switch 2 dock is designed to resemble the Nintendo 64, offering a nostalgic aesthetic. It can hold six Game Cards in its cartridge slot. The 64BITDOCK is available now starting at $89.
insight: Retro gaming meets modern convenience in nostalgic Nintendo 64-inspired design.

title: Enthusiast reverse-engineers Steam Controller 2 puck, creates DIY 'OpenPuck' that works without Steam Input custom firmware can emulate Nintendo, PlayStation, and Xbox controllers
summary: A DIY Steam Controller puck, OpenPuck, can emulate Xbox, PlayStation, and Switch controllers without Steam Input custom firmware, allowing users to bypass the need for Steam to be open in the background.
insight: Gaming flexibility without proprietary software dependencies.

title: Reverse-engineering brings RK3576 NPU support to open-source Rocket driver for mainline Linux
summary: The open-source Rocket driver for mainline Linux now supports the Rockchip RK3576 NPU after reverse-engineering, thanks to Ga Hing Woo's efforts, which have delivered decent performance for object detection on the Radxa ROCK 4D with Linux 7.1-rc5.
insight: RK3576 NPU support expands open-source driver capabilities and performance.

title: $50 Forgix board pairs Raspberry Pi RP2354 MCU with Trion T8 FPGA in Teensy form factor
summary: The Forgix board combines Raspberry Pi RP2354 MCU and Efinix Trion T8 FPGA in a Teensy form factor, offering a unique platform for development and prototyping.
insight: Unique combination of MCU and FPGA in a compact, affordable board.

title: DigiKey video series focuses on Sustainable Futures
summary: DigiKey, Analog, and Harlan collaborate on a video series highlighting the role of sensors and components in achieving sustainable futures through improved power efficiency. The series showcases innovations driving a massive shift towards environmentally conscious electronics. This initiative aims to empower creators and designers with knowledge and tools.
insight: Energy-efficient electronics reduce environmental impact and costs significantly.

title: Testing AMD's EXPO Ultra Low Latency Memory
summary: AMD's EXPO Ultra Low Latency memory offers improved performance without manual tuning. G.Skill's latest DDR5 kits were tested on Ryzen systems for gaming benefits. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of EXPO technology.
insight: Faster memory timings boost Ryzen gaming performance without manual calibration.

title: SoftBank boss says the AI revolution will cost $5 trillion a year, also, there's no bubble
summary: SoftBank's founder Masayoshi Son predicts $5 trillion annual AI spending, dismissing bubble fears.
insight: Massive AI investment to reshape global economy dramatically.

title: Samsung launches new SSD 990: reviews praise the performance, question the price
summary: Samsung's new SSD 990 offers improved performance but faces pricing concerns.
insight: Expensive launch price may impact consumer adoption and market share.

title: HWMonitor restores hotspot temperature monitoring for RTX 50 GPUs
summary: HWMonitor 1.65 restored hotspot temperature monitoring for RTX 50 GPUs, providing users with the hottest point on the GPU die temperature instead of just the average core temperature.
insight: Precision temperature tracking helps diagnose thermal issues on RTX 50 GPUs

title: The Blood of Dawnwalker director says delayed next-gen consoles could benefit smaller studios
summary: The Blood of Dawnwalker director believes delays in next-gen consoles could benefit small studios, allowing them to focus on optimizing their games for existing consoles.
insight: Delays give small studios a window to optimize for existing consoles.

title: Windows Search is getting the cleanup users have wanted for years (more like decades)
summary: Windows Search is getting a long-overdue update, simplifying its home screen and removing unnecessary features.
insight: Simplifying search interface improves user experience and productivity significantly immediately.

title: Christopher Nolan believes Gen Z will push back against generative AI and its growing influence
summary: Christopher Nolan expressed concerns about generative AI's growing influence on society, predicting that Gen Z will push back against it.
insight: Youthful rebellion may challenge AI's increasing presence in modern life.

title: Intel's Starfire SoC combines 18A CPU, Intel 3 GPU, and NPU for space-grade computing
summary: Intel's Starfire SoC combines an 8-core 18A CPU with an Intel 3 GPU and NPU for space-grade computing, featuring separate performance and power efficiency models.
insight: Advancements in space-grade computing for improved satellite and space exploration capabilities.

title: China's courts say your digital game items are inheritable Steam and The West disagree
summary: China's courts recognize game account inheritability, differing from Steam's stance.
insight: In-game purchases raise questions about ownership and digital legacy rights.

title: Black Flag Resynced is a smash hit, but 51 Ubisoft Barcelona developers are still being laid off
summary: Ubisoft's new game Black Flag Resynced is a hit, but layoffs continue. 51 developers at Ubisoft Barcelona face layoffs, part of a larger wave of over 380 job cuts across the company. This includes offices in Winnipeg and Belgrade, as well as the global publishing division.
insight: Game success doesn't guarantee job security in the gaming industry.

title: UChicago Law bans laptops and phones for first-year students, mandates in-class exams starting this fall
summary: The University of Chicago Law School bans laptops and phones for first-year students, mandating in-class exams starting this fall in response to AI disruptions in higher education.
insight: AI adoption forces law schools to adapt and innovate their education methods.

title: GigaWiper malware can wipe your hard drive, spy on you, and stream your screen live
summary: GigaWiper malware combines multiple families, posing a significant threat to systems, enabling data wiping, surveillance, and live screen streaming.
insight: Combining malware families creates an incredibly powerful and stealthy threat vector.

title: Sega's Virtua Racing hits PC and Xbox for the first time, with 8-player online
summary: Sega's Virtua Racing, a classic F1-style arcade racer, is now available on PC and Xbox with online multiplayer support for up to 8 players.
insight: Historic arcade game makes its PC and Xbox debut with online capabilities.

title: AMD's Zen 6 Epyc chip packs 256 cores, promises up to 1.7x faster AI
summary: AMD's Zen 6 Epyc chip packs 256 cores, promising up to 1.7x faster AI performance.
insight: Architectural changes drive speed gains over core count increase.

title: OnePlus could be exiting the US and European markets as soon as this week
summary: OnePlus may exit US and European markets due to reported global withdrawal plans.
insight: OnePlus' market withdrawal could impact global smartphone sales dynamics significantly.

title: Black Flag Resynced is a hit, but its always-online requirement just proved it's not really offline
summary: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced became unplayable due to Ubisoft Connect outage, despite having offline mode.
insight: Always-online requirement undermines offline gaming experience promises.

title: The studio behind MindsEye is flying fans in for playtests, all while laid-off workers protested outside
summary: The studio behind MindsEye invited fans to playtests in Scotland with flights and accommodations covered.
insight: Company engages with community through targeted and inclusive playtesting opportunities.
